text:
Market today: Chip stocks are feeling the heat, thanks to external volatility from SK Hynix. On top of that, there's a big red flag flashing about AI-related debt, which has nearly doubled in a year – a potential "shock to the system" for tech investors like you.

Your holdings: SOXL is down a painful 35.02% and SPAL is down 34%, both leveraged ETFs flagged for attention. QQQI is also on "TRIM" status, down 1.77% and nearing its stop. Remember, margin amplifies these drops, especially with leveraged products that also decay.

Watch: Keep a close eye on SOXL and SPAL's stops, as well as QQQI. Also, watch for any further fallout in the broader chip sector from the SK Hynix news and how AI debt concerns play out for your tech names.

Bottom line: With leveraged positions and sector-specific headwinds, aggressive risk management, especially respecting stops on your high-risk holdings, is paramount right now.
plays: SOXL: Given "SELL" status and -35.02% loss, consider reducing exposure to this leveraged chip ETF.
SPAL: With a -34% loss and "NEW" status, consider trimming this leveraged ETF to manage risk.
QQQI: Respect the stop at 52; its "TRIM" status and being close to this level warrant attention.
